steps.title: Blend
steps.text: Curd, 200ml cold water, jeera, kala namak, mint, chilli. Blitz 20 seconds.
steps.cue: Frothy top, pale green flecks

steps.title: Pour tall
steps.text: Over ice if it is that kind of day. Roasted jeera dusted on the foam.
steps.cue: Foam holds for a few seconds - that is the blend working

rescues.fix: More cold water and a pinch of salt evens it out
You skipped roasting the jeera - it is the whole personality

host.plating: Steel tumbler if you are honest, tall glass if you are hosting
host.prep_ahead: Blend a jug before dinner, stir before pouring

lore: Ayurveda has prescribed chaas as a post-meal digestive for a few thousand years. Your grandmother enforced it considerably more effectively.
Buttermilk technically means the liquid left after churning butter - which is why chaas is naturally lighter than lassi, its full-fat cousin.

name: Nimbu Soda
tagline: Fizz, lime, and the exact taste of an Indian summer.

variants.name: Salted
variants.swaps: Skip sugar, double the kala namak

variants.name: Jaljeera-spiced
variants.swaps: Add roasted jeera, mint, and a pinch of amchur

steps.title: Dissolve
steps.text: Lime, sugar, kala namak in the glass with one spoon of soda. Stir till the sugar is gone.
steps.cue: No grit at the bottom

steps.title: Top
steps.text: Ice, then soda poured down the side. One lazy stir, no more.
steps.cue: Fizz climbs but does not overflow - pour slower than you want to

rescues.fix: Sugar was undissolved when soda hit - always dissolve first
A splash more soda and a pinch of sugar rounds it

leftovers.verdict: Drink now, fizz waits for no one

host.call_it: fresh lime soda, sweet and salted
host.plating: Tall glass, lime wheel, salted rim if you are showing off
host.prep_ahead: Lime-sugar base in a jug, soda per glass on demand

lore: Every Indian railway platform has sold a version of this since the 1900s - the banta, sealed with a glass marble, is its street-fighter cousin.
The sweet-salt-citrus combo is basically an electrolyte formula, which is why it beats imported sports drinks at their own game for a tenth of the price.

name: Cucumber-Mint Cooler
tagline: A spa day you can drink.
lore: Cucumbers are 96% water, which makes this less a recipe and more a phase change with garnish.
The salt is doing electrolyte work - this is quietly the best post-workout, pre-anything summer drink on the board.

steps.title: Blend and strain
steps.text: Cucumber, mint, lime, salt with a splash of water. Blitz, strain through a sieve into the glass.
steps.cue: Pale green juice, foam on top

steps.title: Top with soda
steps.text: Ice, soda down the side, cucumber wheel on the rim.
steps.cue: Two green layers that merge on the first stir

rescues.fix: Mint stems got blended - leaves only, and more lime
Cucumber skin was waxy or bitter - peel it next round

leftovers.reheat: Add soda per glass, never store fizzed

host.call_it: cucumber cooler, no bad decisions in it
host.plating: Tall glass, cucumber ribbon inside the glass wall
host.prep_ahead: Blend the base before guests, soda to order

name: Cold Brew Tonic
tagline: Coffee and tonic - trust the weird, it works.
lore: Born in Scandinavian and Japanese specialty cafes in the 2010s - baristas discovered quinine's bitterness frames coffee's fruitiness like nothing else.
Pour the coffee over the tonic, never the reverse - carbonation collapses if the coffee lands first.

ingredients.display: 2 tsp instant coffee in 50ml cold water (or 60ml real cold brew)

steps.title: Tonic first
steps.text: Glass packed with ice, tonic in, leave two fingers of headroom.
steps.cue: Bubbles racing up the glass wall

steps.title: Float the coffee
steps.text: Pour the coffee slowly over the back of a spoon so it layers.
steps.cue: Dark amber floating on pale gold - do not stir, let the drinker do it

rescues.fix: Coffee was warm - it must be fully cold or the layers and the flavor both collapse
The lemon is load-bearing - squeeze it in, it bridges the two

host.plating: Clear highball, layers visible, lemon wheel riding the ice
host.prep_ahead: Cold coffee batch in the fridge, tonic to order

name: Virgin Mojito
tagline: All of the party, none of the paperwork.
lore: The mojito's ancestor was medicine - lime and mint masked the burn of rough cane spirit for 1500s Cuban sailors. Skip the spirit and the medicine still works.
Muddle means press, not pulverize - shredded mint releases chlorophyll and turns the drink bitter and swampy.

ingredients.display: 8-10 mint leaves plus a crown for the top
juice of most of a lime, plus a wedge inside

steps.title: Press
steps.text: Mint, sugar, lime in the glass. Press with the back of a spoon 8-10 times, no more.
steps.cue: You smell mint without seeing torn leaves

steps.title: Build
steps.text: Crushed ice to the top, soda in, one lift with the spoon from the bottom.
steps.cue: Leaves distribute up the glass like a snow globe

rescues.fix: Over-muddled - strain into a fresh glass over new ice with extra soda
Dissolve sugar in the lime juice before the ice goes in

host.call_it: virgin mojito, nobody misses the rum
host.plating: Tall glass, mint crown slapped awake before it goes on
host.prep_ahead: Lime-sugar-mint base pressed in glasses ahead, ice and soda live

name: Iced Lemon Tea
tagline: No sugar, all citrus, the afternoon reset.
lore: Double-strength brew because ice is a dilution tax - same rule as iced coffee.

ingredients.display: 2 green tea bags or 2 tsp loose

steps.title: Brew strong
steps.text: Steep tea 5 min in 200ml hot water. Remove bags.

steps.title: Chill fast
steps.text: Pour over ice, lemon squeeze. Drink before it turns watery.

rescues.fix: Green tea oversteeped - 3 min max next time
Brew double bags; ice needs bold tea

name: Protein Cold Coffee
tagline: The cafe order, minus the sugar syrup economics.

variants.name: Mocha
variants.swaps: Chocolate whey plus a pinch of cocoa

steps.title: Blend hard
steps.text: Everything with a handful of ice, 30 seconds on high.
steps.cue: Foam cap like a cafe frappe

steps.title: Pour
steps.text: Tall glass, straight away, while the foam stands.
steps.cue: Foam holds a dusting of coffee powder

rescues.fix: Blender, not spoon - and cold milk always
Flavored whey carries it - or 2 dates blended in

lore: Indian cafe cold coffee is a milkshake in a trench coat - easily 400 kcal of sugar and ice cream. This build keeps the ritual, swaps the payload.
Caffeine plus protein is a legitimate pre-workout stack - the gym bros were right about this one.

name: Salted Lassi, Light
tagline: Thicker chaas, same soul, extra protein optional.
lore: Salted lassi is chaas's thicker cousin - less water, more curd, same kala namak logic.

steps.title: Blend thick
steps.text: Curd, minimal water, whey, kala namak, jeera, mint. 30 sec.

steps.title: Pour cold
steps.text: Over ice if needed. Foam is correct.

rescues.fix: Less water - lassi should coat the glass
Use unflavored whey and more jeera

name: Whiskey Highball
tagline: The leanest real drink there is.

steps.title: Build
steps.text: Fill a highball glass with ice. Pour whiskey, top with soda, squeeze lime wedge.
steps.cue: Glass frosts on the outside

steps.title: Stir once
steps.text: One gentle stir, do not overwork.
steps.cue: Lime and whiskey swirl but do not mix fully

rescues.fix: More soda - build is 1:3 whiskey to soda, 1:4 works too
Fresh lime is load-bearing, do not skip

host.plating: Highball glass, visible ice, lime wheel on rim
host.prep_ahead: Ice and soda ready, build when asked

lore: Japan built an entire bar culture on this two-ingredient drink - Suntory's 2000s highball revival is credited with saving whisky for a generation of drinkers.
Alcohol runs 7 kcal per gram, parked between carbs and fat - and the highball is the leanest delivery system ever engineered: whiskey, bubbles, lime, done.

name: Gin and Tonic
tagline: Two ingredients, four centuries of field testing.
lore: The tonic was the point: quinine was the British Raj's anti-malaria med, and it tasted so vile that officers in India cut it with gin, sugar and lime. The cure outlived the disease.
Gin is legally vodka that went to finishing school - juniper must lead the flavor, everything after that is the distiller showing off. The cucumber build is why this card skips the lime wheel.

variants.name: Citrus build
variants.swaps: Lime wedge squeezed and dropped in, skip cucumber

ingredients.display: 150ml diet tonic - regular tonic adds 35 sneaky kcal
one crack of pepper over the top - trust it

steps.title: Chill everything
steps.text: Big ice, lots of it - a full glass melts slower than a half glass. Gin over.
steps.cue: Glass fogs

steps.title: Tonic down the side
steps.text: Pour slow against the glass wall to keep the fizz. Cucumber in, pepper crack.
steps.cue: Bubbles bead on the cucumber ribbons

rescues.fix: Warm glass or hard pour - freeze the glass, pour like it is nitroglycerin
Ratio drifted - 1:3 gin to tonic is home, recalibrate

host.plating: Biggest glass you own, absurd amounts of ice, ribbons visible
host.prep_ahead: Glasses in the freezer an hour before guests

name: Margarita, Fresh Lime
tagline: No mix, no sugar rim, no apologies.
lore: At least five people claimed they invented it for a woman named Margarita, and history believes none of them. What is certain: bottled margarita mix is why people think they hate tequila.
Real tequila says '100% agave' on the label - anything else is a 'mixto', padded with cane sugar, and it is the difference between a warm evening and a difficult morning.

variants.name: Spicy rim
variants.swaps: Salt mixed with chilli powder on half the rim only - you will want the off-ramp

ingredients.display: juice of 1 fat lime (30ml), squeezed in the last hour
ingredients.sub: bottled juice is the enemy of this drink

ingredients.display: 7g honey (1 tsp) shaken to dissolve - the agave-syrup stand-in
salt for half the rim, lime-wetted

steps.title: Rim half the glass
steps.text: Lime wedge around half the rim, dip in salt. Half, so every sip is a choice.
steps.cue: Thin, even crust - a salt cliff ruins sips

steps.title: Shake hard
steps.text: Tequila, lime, honey, ice. Shake 15 seconds like it owes you money.
steps.cue: Shaker frosts over and hurts to hold

steps.title: Strain over fresh ice
steps.text: Into the glass, lime wheel on top.
steps.cue: Cloudy, pale, tiny ice shards on the surface

rescues.fix: Your limes were angrier than mine - another half tsp honey, reshake
Shaken too long or weak ice - bigger cubes, 15 seconds max

host.call_it: fresh lime margarita - the mix stays in the store
host.plating: Rocks glass, half salt rim, lime wheel
host.prep_ahead: Juice limes and pre-mix tequila-honey; shake per round with ice

name: Spritz
tagline: Golden hour, poured over ice.
lore: Austrian soldiers stationed in 1800s Veneto found the local wine too strong and asked for it 'gespritzt' - sprayed with water. The Italians, appalled, eventually made it beautiful instead.
Aperol is only 11% alcohol - half of Campari's strength and twice its sunset color. The 3-2-1 build (prosecco-Aperol-soda) is codified enough that Venice bartenders will correct you.

ingredients.sub: Campari for the grown-up bitter version

ingredients.display: fat orange slice, in the glass not on the rim

steps.title: Ice first, wine second
steps.text: Big glass, lots of ice, prosecco before Aperol - it keeps the bubbles alive.
steps.cue: Pale gold before the sunset arrives

steps.title: Aperol and soda
steps.text: Aperol slides down and layers. Splash of soda, orange slice in, one stir.
steps.cue: The color gradient is the garnish

rescues.fix: Too much soda or sweet prosecco - rebuild 3-2-1 with a brut
That is the Aperol talking - one extra splash of prosecco softens the landing

leftovers.verdict: Build per glass, prosecco waits for no one

host.plating: Big wine glass, orange slice standing in the ice
host.prep_ahead: Chill everything - the build itself is 30 seconds of theater

name: Mojito, Light Sugar
tagline: Havana's answer to a hot evening, sugar dialed to honest.
lore: Sir Francis Drake's crew drank the prototype ('El Draque') in the 1500s as scurvy medicine - lime for the vitamin C, mint and cane spirit for morale.

White rum is aged rum with the color filtered out - Cuba built Bacardi on exactly this style because it disappears politely behind lime and mint. Bar mojitos run 250+ kcal; measured sugar brings it home at half.

ingredients.display: juice of most of a lime
5g sugar (heaped tsp) - half the bar build

steps.title: Press the mint
steps.text: Mint, sugar, lime in the glass. Eight gentle presses with a spoon.
steps.cue: Mint fragrant, leaves intact

steps.title: Rum and ice
steps.text: Rum in, crushed ice to the brim, stir once from the bottom.
steps.cue: Leaves rise through the glass

steps.title: Top
steps.text: Soda to fill, mint crown slapped between your palms and planted.
steps.cue: The slap wakes the mint oils - it is not for show. Well, mostly

rescues.fix: Mint was pulverized - strain and rebuild over fresh ice
Crushed ice melted fast - colder soda, less stirring

host.plating: Tall glass, forest of mint on top, two straws never one
host.prep_ahead: Mint pressed in glasses before guests, rum-soda live

name: Old Fashioned
tagline: The drink the word cocktail was invented for.
lore: The 1806 definition of 'cocktail' - spirit, sugar, water, bitters - IS this drink. Everything since is a remix. Ordering one 'old fashioned' was literally asking the bartender to stop being clever.

Bourbon must be 51%+ corn and aged in NEW charred oak every time - Scotland happily buys the barrels America can only use once. The orange peel finish: express the oils over the glass, skin side down, then drop it in.

ingredients.display: 60ml bourbon or good Indian single malt (2 pegs)
4g sugar (1 tsp) dissolved in a few drops of water

ingredients.sub: no real sub - a tiny bottle lasts years

steps.title: Dissolve
steps.text: Sugar, bitters, a bar spoon of water in the glass. Stir till fully dissolved - grit ruins the last sip.
steps.cue: Syrup slicks the bottom of the glass

steps.title: Whiskey and one big cube
steps.text: Whiskey in, the biggest ice you can make, stir 20 slow seconds.
steps.cue: Drink turns silky and chills without clouding

steps.title: Express the peel
steps.text: Pinch the orange peel skin-down over the surface, wipe the rim, drop it in.
steps.cue: A fine citrus mist catches the light - that is the garnish working

rescues.fix: Under-stirred - 10 more seconds of dilution is the difference
Small ice - one giant cube melts slow, a handful of pebbles is soup

host.plating: Heavy rocks glass, one enormous cube, peel riding the ice
host.prep_ahead: Freeze big cubes the night before - they are the whole ceremony

name: Sangria Pitcher
tagline: Wine that brought fruit to the party.
lore: Sangria means 'bloodletting' in Spanish - named for the color, not the morning after. Spaniards drank watered, fruited wine for centuries because the water itself was the dangerous part.

The macerating fruit does the sweetening, which is why this pitcher skips added sugar entirely - four hours in the fridge and the orange has done the work a cup of syrup does in bad bar versions. Macros shown per glass; pitcher serves 4.

ingredients.display: 500ml red wine - young and cheap is CORRECT here (makes 4 glasses)
ingredients.sub: white wine for a sangria blanca

ingredients.display: 1 orange, half juiced, half in wheels
handful of berries or chopped apple
ingredients.sub: any fruit that will not dissolve

ingredients.display: 200ml soda, added at serving time only

steps.title: Macerate
steps.text: Wine, juice, all the fruit into a pitcher. Fridge, 4 hours minimum, overnight is the pro move.
steps.cue: Fruit stains the wine's edges - the flavors have merged

steps.title: Serve
steps.text: Soda in at the last minute, ice in the glasses not the pitcher, fruit into every pour.
steps.cue: Each glass gets wine, fizz, and fruit - the trifecta

rescues.fix: It did not macerate long enough - patience is an ingredient here
Soda went into the pitcher - always per-glass

leftovers.verdict: Fruit-wine keeps a day, gets stronger

host.call_it: sangria - the pitcher does the hosting
host.plating: Glass pitcher, wooden spoon for fruit rescue, stack of tumblers
host.prep_ahead: Built the night before by design - this is the zero-effort party pour

name: Espresso Martini
tagline: Wake me up, then mess me up.
lore: Invented by Dick Bradsell at a London bar in 1983 when a young model asked for something to - his words - 'wake me up, then mess me up'. He was next to the coffee machine. History was made.

Vodka's whole legal identity is neutrality - distilled to taste of nothing - which is why the coffee does all the talking here. The foam crown is coffee oils whipped by a violent shake, no cream involved. Three beans on top: health, wealth, happiness.

ingredients.sub: skip it, add 1 tsp sugar - leaner and still great

ingredients.display: 30ml strong fresh espresso (or 1.5 tsp instant in 30ml hot water, cooled)

steps.title: Coffee first, cooled
steps.text: Brew strong, let it stop steaming. Hot coffee murders the ice and the foam.
steps.cue: Warm, not hot, to the touch

steps.title: Shake violently
steps.text: Everything with lots of ice. Twenty seconds, harder than feels reasonable - the foam is made here.
steps.cue: Shaker sounds different when the foam forms - duller, heavier

steps.title: Double strain
steps.text: Through a sieve into a chilled coupe or small glass. Three coffee beans on the crema.
steps.cue: A centimeter of dense tan foam that holds the beans

rescues.fix: Weak shake or warm coffee - both fixable, neither negotiable
The liqueur did it - halve it next round and let the espresso lead

host.call_it: espresso martini - dessert and a plan
host.plating: Chilled coupe, three beans, served the second the foam settles
host.prep_ahead: Brew and chill the coffee before guests - shaking is the show

name: Negroni
tagline: Equal parts bitter, sweet, and conviction.
lore: Florence, 1919: Count Camillo Negroni asked the bartender to stiffen his Americano by swapping the soda for gin. The bartender switched the lemon garnish to orange so waiters could tell them apart. Both decisions stuck for a century.

Campari's exact recipe is known to allegedly ten people, and its famous red came from crushed cochineal insects until 2006. This card pours 25ml of each - the classic 30ml build lands at 185 kcal, and honestly, some nights earn it.

variants.name: Negroni sbagliato
variants.swaps: Prosecco instead of gin - 'sbagliato' means 'mistaken', invented by exactly that

ingredients.sub: Aperol for training wheels - sweeter, gentler

ingredients.display: 25ml sweet vermouth (fridge after opening - it is wine, it dies on the shelf)

steps.title: Build over ice
steps.text: All three into a rocks glass over one big cube. Equal parts - the recipe is the ratio.
steps.cue: Deep ruby red before the stir

steps.title: Stir and garnish
steps.text: Fifteen slow seconds. Orange expressed over the top and in.
steps.cue: First sip should be cold, bitter, and slightly shocking - it grows on you by sip three

rescues.fix: You are not wrong, you are just early - try the sbagliato variant and work up
Dead vermouth - if the bottle is over 2 months open and unrefrigerated, it is vinegar now

host.call_it: negroni, equal parts and no debate
host.plating: Rocks glass, one big cube, orange peel - it photographs like a stained-glass window
host.prep_ahead: Batch the three bottles 1:1:1 in a jar; pour over ice per guest

name: Moscow Mule
tagline: Ginger with a kick and a copper agenda.
lore: Invented in 1941 Los Angeles by three people with three problems: unsellable vodka, unsellable ginger beer, and a warehouse of copper mugs. The mule moved all three. Marketing has never been this honest since.

The copper mug is thermal theater - it frost-transfers cold to your hand and lips. Real ginger beer is brewed and cloudy; ginger ale is soda wearing a costume. The diet ginger ale sub saves 25 kcal and loses the burn.

ingredients.display: 120ml ginger beer, the spiciest you can find
juice of half a lime, shell dropped in
2 thin coins of fresh ginger, optional overachieving

steps.title: Build cold
steps.text: Copper mug if you have one, any glass if you do not. Packed ice, vodka, lime juice and the spent shell.
steps.cue: Mug frosts white where the ice sits

steps.title: Ginger beer top
steps.text: Pour down the side, one stir, ginger coins on top.
steps.cue: The ginger burn should reach your nose before the glass reaches your lips

rescues.problem: Tastes like soda water with regret
rescues.fix: Weak ginger beer - it carries the whole drink, buy the fierce one
A tsp of honey syrup rounds the ginger without killing it

host.call_it: Moscow mule - copper optional, cold mandatory
host.plating: Copper mug, lime shell floating, ginger coin on the ice
host.prep_ahead: Mugs in the freezer, lime juiced - assembly is 30 seconds

name: Paloma
tagline: Mexico's actual favorite - the margarita is for tourists.
lore: Ask a bartender in Guadalajara for tequila's true partner and it is grapefruit soda, not lime and salt. The paloma ('dove') outsells the margarita across Mexico and has never needed a franchise.

Tequila can only legally come from five Mexican states, same energy as Champagne - everything else agave-based is mezcal's extended family. The grapefruit's gentle bitterness is doing the same job tonic does for gin.

ingredients.display: 100ml grapefruit juice, fresh or unsweetened boxed
pinch of salt in the drink itself - the secret

steps.title: Salt inside, not on the rim
steps.text: Tall glass, ice, tequila, grapefruit, lime, and the pinch of salt stirred in.
steps.cue: Salt disappears but the grapefruit tastes twice as bright

steps.title: Soda lift
steps.text: Top with soda, one stir, grapefruit wedge if you are plating.
steps.cue: Pale pink, fizzing, faintly cloudy

rescues.fix: Your grapefruit juice was harsh - a tsp of honey syrup fixes it faster than sugar
You skipped the salt pinch - it is the entire trick

host.plating: Tall glass, grapefruit wedge, no rim theatrics
host.prep_ahead: Juice ahead; it is a 30-second build per guest

name: Whiskey Sour
tagline: Silk foam on a sour - the egg white is the plot twist.
lore: The sour formula - spirit, citrus, sugar - was standard-issue on 1800s ships because scurvy was a worse hangover. The egg white came later, from bartenders chasing texture, and it adds silk, not egg flavor. This is also the rare cocktail with 3g of protein, which changes nothing but amuses everyone.
The 'dry shake' - shaking without ice first - is the foam's whole secret: proteins whip better warm. Then ice, then shake again. Skip it and the crown never forms.

ingredients.display: 8g sugar (2 tsp) as a quick syrup
ingredients.sub: honey - it becomes a Gold Rush, also excellent
15ml aquafaba (chickpea water) for the same foam

ingredients.display: 1 dash bitters on the foam, dragged into art

steps.title: Dry shake
steps.text: Everything except ice into the shaker. Shake 15 seconds - this builds the foam.
steps.cue: Shaker feels lighter as the foam expands inside

steps.title: Wet shake
steps.text: Ice in, shake 15 more to chill and dilute.
steps.cue: Frost on the tin

steps.title: Strain and paint
steps.text: Strain into a rocks glass over one cube. Dash bitters on the foam, drag a toothpick through.
steps.cue: A centimeter of white silk that holds the bitters swirl

rescues.fix: You skipped or rushed the dry shake - it is non-negotiable
Old egg - fresh whites are odorless in the glass; the lemon covers the rest

host.plating: Rocks glass, bitters art on the crown - it looks like latte art's cool cousin
host.prep_ahead: Make sugar syrup ahead; the double shake is live theater

aka: boston sour (that is this, with the egg white)

name: Espresso
tagline: Thirty seconds of pressure, a lifetime of opinions.
lore: Real espresso is 9 bars of pressure through 18g of coffee in 30 seconds - the crema on top is coffee oils emulsified by force. The moka pot or strong instant gets you 80% of the way for 2% of the machine cost.

ingredients.display: 2 tsp strong instant in 60ml just-off-boil water (or a proper double shot)
ingredients.sub: moka pot brew, closer to the real thing

steps.title: Bloom and stir
steps.text: Coffee in the smallest cup you own, water over, stir ten seconds till a thin foam forms.
steps.cue: A tan ring of foam - your instant crema

rescues.fix: Water too cool - it must be just off the boil

leftovers.verdict: Drink it standing, like in Naples

name: Americano
tagline: Espresso, diluted with hot water and dignity.
lore: Named for American GIs in WWII Italy who found espresso too intense and watered it down. The Italians meant it as an insult. The Americans took it as a menu item.

ingredients.display: 1.5 tsp instant (or a double shot) in 200ml hot water

steps.title: Water first, coffee over
steps.text: Hot water in the mug, coffee concentrate poured on top - preserves the aroma layer.
steps.cue: Foam floats instead of dissolving

rescues.fix: Old instant - keep the jar sealed, buy small

name: Cappuccino
tagline: One-third coffee, two-thirds cloud.
lore: Named after Capuchin monks, whose brown robes matched the color of espresso cut with milk. Italians consider one after 11am a crime against digestion; the rest of the world has agreed to disagree.

ingredients.display: 1.5 tsp instant in 40ml hot water - the shot

steps.title: Froth
steps.text: Heat milk to steaming (not boiling), then whisk, pump in a french press, or shake in a hot-safe jar till doubled.
steps.cue: Foam holds a spoon's weight for a second

steps.title: Pour thirds
steps.text: Shot in the cup, milk in, spoon the foam cap on top.
steps.cue: White dome standing above the rim

rescues.fix: Milk boiled - 65C is the ceiling, steaming not bubbling

name: Flat White
tagline: A latte that went to art school and got serious.
lore: Australia and New Zealand have fought over who invented it since the 1980s and neither will ever concede. The difference from a latte: double the coffee, half the foam, milk textured to wet paint.

ingredients.display: 2 tsp instant in 40ml hot water - a double
130ml milk, steamed silky with barely any foam

steps.title: Texture, not froth
steps.text: Heat milk and whisk gently - you want gloss, not bubbles.
steps.cue: Surface like wet paint, no visible foam

steps.title: Pour through the shot
steps.text: Steady pour into the coffee from a height, finishing low.
steps.cue: Thin white ribbon settles into tan

rescues.problem: It is just a small latte
rescues.fix: Correct. The coffee dose is what makes it a flat white - do not skimp the double

name: Hot Mocha
tagline: The peace treaty between coffee and chocolate.
lore: Named after Mokha, the Yemeni port that ran the world's coffee trade for 200 years - its beans had a natural chocolate note, and when trade moved on, bartenders faked the note with actual chocolate.

steps.title: Paste first
steps.text: Cocoa, coffee, sugar with a splash of hot water - stir to a glossy paste. Lumps die here or never.
steps.cue: Smooth dark syrup

steps.title: Milk in, low heat
steps.text: Whisk milk in over low flame till steaming.
steps.cue: First wisp of steam, tiny bubbles at the edge

rescues.fix: You skipped the paste step - strain it and remember for next time

name: South Indian Filter Coffee
tagline: The metre-long pour is not optional.
lore: The blend is coffee with 10-30% chicory - a French wartime stretch that South India kept because it makes the brew thicker and sweeter. The tumbler-to-dabara pour aerates, cools, and dissolves the sugar all at once; the longer the arc, the better the froth and the show.

ingredients.display: 8g filter coffee powder (with chicory), packed in the filter
ingredients.sub: dark instant at half quantity, lesser but legal

ingredients.display: 1 tsp sugar - kaapi is not the place for restraint

steps.title: Slow drip
steps.text: Powder in the upper chamber, press disc on, boiling water over. Walk away 15 minutes - decoction cannot be rushed.
steps.cue: Thick dark decoction in the lower chamber

steps.title: The pour
steps.text: Decoction plus hot milk plus sugar, then pour between tumbler and dabara from increasing height.
steps.cue: A finger of froth on top - that is the metre working

rescues.fix: Powder too coarse or water not boiling - and never reuse the bed

leftovers.verdict: Decoction keeps a day in the fridge
leftovers.reheat: Add to fresh hot milk, never microwave the mix

name: Masala Chai
tagline: The subcontinent's operating system.
lore: Saying 'chai tea' means saying 'tea tea' - chai IS the word for tea. The masala part came from Ayurvedic spice mixes hitched to the British tea habit, and the railway 'cutting' (half glass) exists so you can afford to say yes every single time.

variants.name: Sick-day build
variants.swaps: Double ginger, add 4 crushed black peppercorns and a pinch of ajwain

ingredients.display: 1.5 tsp strong black tea (Assam CTC does the heavy lifting)
half inch ginger, crushed not sliced

steps.title: Boil the spices
steps.text: Ginger and cardamom in 120ml water, rolling boil for 2 minutes before the tea goes in.
steps.cue: Water faintly gold, kitchen smells like a tapri

steps.title: Tea, then milk, then patience
steps.text: Tea in for 1 minute, milk and sugar in, then bring it up to the rim and back down twice.
steps.cue: The rise-and-settle is where the color deepens to terracotta

rescues.fix: Spices went in with the milk - they must boil in water first, milk mutes extraction

leftovers.verdict: Fresh only - reboiled chai is a war crime

name: Hot Matcha Latte
tagline: Stone-ground calm in a cup.
lore: Matcha is whole tea leaves, shade-grown, stone-ground - so you drink the leaf instead of steeping it, caffeine plus L-theanine, which is why the buzz lands smooth instead of jittery. Whisk in a W, never a circle, and 80C water max: boiling water burns it bitter.

ingredients.display: 3g matcha (1 heaped tsp), sifted - lumps are the enemy

steps.title: Whisk the paste
steps.text: Sifted matcha with 30ml of 80C water. Whisk in a W motion till frothy - a small regular whisk works.
steps.cue: Fine foam, no visible powder, jade green

steps.title: Milk over
steps.text: Hot milk and honey in, one gentle stir.
steps.cue: Even pale green, foam intact

rescues.fix: Water was boiling - 80C means kettle rested 2 minutes
You skipped the sift - always the sift

name: Protein Hot Chocolate
tagline: Dessert impersonating a recovery drink, or maybe the reverse.
lore: The Aztecs drank chocolate bitter, spiced, and cold, and used the beans as currency - meaning you could literally drink your money. The whey here is the modern trick: it thickens like custard and pushes 20g protein into a mug of dessert.

ingredients.display: 1 square dark chocolate, for the melt

steps.title: Cocoa paste
steps.text: Cocoa with 2 tbsp hot water to a slurry, then milk, low heat till steaming.
steps.cue: Steam, no boil - whey curdles in a boil

steps.title: Off heat, whey and chocolate
steps.text: Flame off, wait 30 seconds, whisk in whey and drop the square in.
steps.cue: Thickens visibly as the whey blooms, square melts on the way down

rescues.fix: Milk was too hot for the whey - always off heat, and whisk while pouring

name: Iced Americano
tagline: Korea's national fuel, available in your kitchen.
lore: Korea drinks these in a blizzard - 'eoljukah' is slang for 'I'll have an iced americano even if I freeze to death'. Pour the hot shot directly over ice: the flash-chill locks the aroma that slow cooling loses.

ingredients.display: 2 tsp instant dissolved hot in 50ml, then flash-chilled over ice with 150ml cold water
optional lemon twist - sounds wrong, tastes right

steps.title: Hot bloom, cold crash
steps.text: Dissolve coffee in the hot water, pour straight over a full glass of ice, top with cold water.
steps.cue: Ice cracks loudly - that is the flash-chill

rescues.fix: More ice, not less - a packed glass melts slower than three sad cubes

name: Iced Latte
tagline: The default order for a reason.
lore: The cafe version costs 300 rupees because of rent, not ingredients - this is 4 rupees of coffee and a splash of milk. Pour the shot over the milk, not the reverse, for the two-tone gradient that does the marketing.

ingredients.display: 2 tsp instant dissolved in 40ml hot water

steps.title: Milk, ice, then the shot on top
steps.text: Cold milk over packed ice, coffee poured slowly over the back of a spoon.
steps.cue: Dark layer floating on white - serve unstirred

rescues.fix: The shot must be strong - 2 tsp in 40ml is syrup-strength on purpose

name: Cold Brew
tagline: Patience, filtered.
lore: Cold water extracts the caffeine and sweetness but leaves most of the bitter acids behind - cold brew runs up to two-thirds less acidic than hot coffee, which is why it tastes like chocolate and why your stomach sends a thank-you note. Dutch traders were making it on ships in the 1600s; it was never new, just rediscovered.

steps.title: Steep overnight
steps.text: Grounds plus 250ml room-temp water in a jar. Stir once, lid on, counter or fridge for 14 hours.
steps.cue: Deep mahogany, smells like chocolate

steps.title: Strain twice
steps.text: Through a fine sieve, then through a cloth or paper filter. Serve over ice, dilute to taste.
steps.cue: Clear, no sludge at the bottom of the glass

rescues.fix: Grind too fine - coarse like sea salt, and the second strain is not optional
It steeps 14 hours minimum - 8 hours is tea with delusions

name: Iced Mocha
tagline: Chocolate milk with a caffeine clearance.
lore: The cafe iced mocha is routinely 400+ kcal of syrup and whipped cream. Cocoa powder plus honey rebuilds the flavor at a quarter of the payload - the chocolate note is real, the regret is optional.

ingredients.display: 1 tbsp cocoa, into the hot shot

steps.title: Mocha base
steps.text: Cocoa and honey whisked into the hot coffee till glossy.
steps.cue: No cocoa specks on the spoon

steps.title: Ice and milk
steps.text: Base over ice, milk over the base.
steps.cue: Marble swirls as it settles

rescues.fix: Cocoa met cold liquid - it only dissolves in the hot shot

name: Vietnamese Iced Coffee
tagline: Strong coffee, sweet milk, zero compromise.
lore: French colonists brought coffee to Vietnam but fresh milk spoiled in the tropics - condensed milk from a can did not, and the workaround became the signature. The phin filter drips slow by design; the wait is part of the drink.

ingredients.display: 2.5 tsp dark instant in 60ml hot water - Vietnamese robusta is STRONG, match it
30g condensed milk (1.5 tbsp) in the glass first
ingredients.sub: no honest sub - this IS the drink

steps.title: Layer
steps.text: Condensed milk at the bottom, hot strong coffee poured gently on top. Do not stir yet.
steps.cue: White sediment under black coffee - two clean layers

steps.title: Stir over ice
steps.text: Packed ice in, now stir hard from the bottom.
steps.cue: Uniform caramel tan - the color of done

rescues.fix: Your coffee was too weak to fight back - the bitterness is a load-bearing wall

name: Dalgona Coffee
tagline: Four hundred whisks to internet immortality.
lore: The 2020 lockdown made it famous via Korea, but Indian households were making 'phenti hui' coffee decades before the hashtag - same whip, no camera. The chemistry: instant coffee is pre-dried extract, so it foams; ground coffee never will.

ingredients.display: 2 tsp instant coffee - must be instant, the foam depends on it
2 tsp sugar - structural, do not halve

steps.title: Whip
steps.text: Coffee, sugar, 2 tsp hot water. Whisk hard 3-4 minutes (or 1 minute electric).
steps.cue: Pale caramel, soft peaks that hold

steps.title: Crown the milk
steps.text: Cold milk over ice, whipped coffee spooned on top.
steps.cue: The cloud floats - stir at the table for the show

rescues.fix: Ground coffee or too much water - instant only, water by the teaspoon

leftovers.verdict: Foam holds an hour in the fridge

host.call_it: dalgona - yes, the lockdown one, it still slaps
host.plating: Clear glass, tall foam cap, spoon standing by
host.prep_ahead: Whip the foam before guests, crown per glass

name: Protein Frappe
tagline: The milkshake that lifts.
lore: The frappe was invented at a Greek trade fair in 1957 by a Nescafe rep who could not find hot water and shook his coffee with cold instead. The whey version is the same accident, upgraded: 25g protein disguised as a milkshake.

variants.name: Mocha frappe
variants.swaps: Add 5g cocoa before blending

ingredients.display: 5g honey, optional if your whey is sweet

steps.title: Blend with the ice
steps.text: Everything plus a fistful of ice, blitz 30 seconds till the blender note changes.
steps.cue: Sound goes from crunch to whir - the slush point

rescues.fix: Not enough ice blended in - the crushed ice is the emulsifier here

name: Affogato
tagline: Dessert drowned on purpose.
lore: Affogato means 'drowned' in Italian, and the entire recipe is the name: hot espresso poured over cold vanilla ice cream. It is the highest ratio of impressiveness to effort on this entire site - two ingredients, thirty seconds, standing ovation.

ingredients.display: 1 scoop good vanilla ice cream, freezer-hard
a hot double shot (1.5 tsp instant in 40ml)

steps.title: Scoop cold, pour hot
steps.text: Scoop into a small glass, hot shot over the top at the table.
steps.cue: Edges melt into caramel rivers while the core stays frozen - eat immediately with a spoon

rescues.fix: Ice cream was soft - it must go glass-to-table straight from the freezer

leftovers.verdict: A concept that cannot be stored

host.call_it: affogato - dessert and coffee in one move
host.plating: Small clear glass, shot poured in front of the guest
host.prep_ahead: Scoop into glasses and hold in the freezer; pour live

name: Shakerato
tagline: Espresso, shaken into a suit and tie.
lore: Italy's answer to iced coffee: shake a hot shot with ice hard enough and it pours with a foam crown like a tiny Guinness - no milk, no cream, pure physics. It is the espresso martini's teetotal grandfather.

ingredients.display: a hot double shot (2 tsp instant in 50ml)
4g honey, dissolved in the hot shot

steps.title: Shake violently
steps.text: Hot sweetened shot plus a full shaker of ice. Fifteen hard seconds.
steps.cue: Shaker frosts and sounds heavier as foam forms

steps.title: Strain high
steps.text: Into a chilled glass, no ice in the glass.
steps.cue: A centimeter of tan crema on top

rescues.fix: Shot was lukewarm or shake was polite - hot shot, violent shake

name: Honey Cinnamon Iced Latte
tagline: The cafe 'signature drink', decoded and undercut.
lore: Every cafe 'signature latte' is the same three-part trick: base coffee, one sweetener, one warm spice. Learn the pattern once and the whole specials board becomes a suggestion.

ingredients.display: 7g honey (1 tsp), dissolved in the hot shot
big pinch of cinnamon in the shot, dusting on top

steps.title: Spiced shot
steps.text: Honey and cinnamon whisked into the hot coffee.
steps.cue: Smells like a bakery, looks like syrup

steps.title: Build
steps.text: Milk over ice, shot over milk, cinnamon dust.
steps.cue: Golden layer bleeding into white

rescues.fix: It went into cold milk - spices only dissolve in the hot shot

name: Coconut Cold Brew
tagline: Beach vacation, no leave application.
lore: Coconut milk's fat carries coffee aroma the way dairy fat does, which is why this works where watery almond attempts fail - the thick top of the can is the barista move.

ingredients.display: cold brew from 12g grounds (see the Cold Brew card), or 2 tsp instant chilled
40ml coconut milk, the thick top of the can

steps.title: Build over ice
steps.text: Cold brew over packed ice, honey stirred in, coconut milk floated on top.
steps.cue: White clouds sinking through dark brew

rescues.fix: Canned milk separated - warm the measured splash 10 seconds and whisk before floating

name: Iced Matcha Latte
tagline: The green drink that earns the aesthetic.
lore: Culinary-grade matcha is fine for lattes - save ceremonial grade for drinking straight, the milk flattens the difference. The layered pour (milk, ice, matcha on top) exists because matcha suspends instead of sinking: the gradient is physics, not filter.

steps.title: Whisk the matcha
steps.text: Sifted matcha, honey, 40ml warm (not hot) water. Whisk in a W till smooth and frothy.
steps.cue: Jade green, fine foam, zero lumps

steps.title: Layer
steps.text: Milk over ice, matcha poured slowly on top.
steps.cue: Green sitting on white - stir at the table

rescues.fix: Cheap matcha or hot water - warm water only, and brands matter here more than anywhere

name: Matcha Lemonade
tagline: Green tea and lemonade filed a joint application.
lore: Lemon juice keeps matcha's chlorophyll bright green instead of oxidizing brown - the vitamin C is a preservative you can taste. This is also the gateway matcha for people who claim they hate matcha.

ingredients.display: 2g matcha, sifted and whisked in 30ml warm water
juice of a third of a lemon

steps.title: Lemon base
steps.text: Honey dissolved in the lemon juice at the bottom of the glass, ice on top, water in.
steps.cue: Cloudy gold lemonade

steps.title: Green crown
steps.text: Whisked matcha poured over the back of a spoon.
steps.cue: Green over gold - the two-tone photo, then stir

rescues.fix: Too much matcha for the lemon - this drink wants 2g, not the latte's 3

name: Sparkling Matcha
tagline: Matcha with bubbles and nowhere to be.
lore: The specialty-cafe cousin of the espresso tonic: quinine's bitterness frames matcha's umami the same way it frames coffee's fruit. Suspended green over clear bubbles - the most photographed drink per rupee on this board.

ingredients.sub: plain soda + a squeeze of lime

steps.title: Tonic first
steps.text: Ice, tonic, leave headroom.
steps.cue: Bubbles climbing the glass

steps.title: Float the green
steps.text: Matcha over the back of a spoon, lime on the rim.
steps.cue: Green cloud hovering - do not stir until seen

rescues.fix: The lime is load-bearing - squeeze it in, it bridges the bitter and the grass

name: Dirty Matcha
tagline: The ceasefire in the coffee-versus-matcha war.
lore: 'Dirty' in cafe language means an espresso shot dropped where it does not belong - and matcha plus espresso is double caffeine with the L-theanine seatbelt still on. Three layers: white milk, green matcha, brown shot. Photograph fast, stir faster.

ingredients.display: a single shot (1.5 tsp instant in 30ml hot water)

steps.title: Milk, then green
steps.text: Milk over ice, whisked matcha floated on it.
steps.cue: Green resting on white

steps.title: The dirty pour
steps.text: Espresso shot dead center, slowly.
steps.cue: Brown bleeding through green into white - three strata

rescues.fix: Poured fast or stirred early - the layers are the point, patience

name: Berry Matcha Latte
tagline: Pink and green and drinking well.
lore: The strawberry matcha's viral career rests on color theory - pink and green are complements, so the glass does the advertising. Frozen berries mashed with honey make a truer, cheaper syrup than anything bottled.

ingredients.display: 40g berries, mashed warm with the honey to a quick compote

steps.title: Berry floor
steps.text: Warm mashed berry-honey compote at the bottom of the glass, ice over.
steps.cue: Pink syrup with whole berry pieces

steps.title: Milk, then matcha
steps.text: Milk over the ice, whisked matcha crowned on top.
steps.cue: Pink, white, green - the full flag, stir at the table

rescues.fix: Compote too thin - mash and reduce 2 minutes, it should be jammy

name: Iced Chai Latte
tagline: Chai that clocked out for the summer.
lore: The cafe-chain 'chai latte' is syrup from a pump; this one is actual chai, brewed double-strength because ice is a dilution tax. Brew it like you mean it and chill it fast.

ingredients.display: 2 tsp strong black tea - double strength on purpose

steps.title: Double-strength brew
steps.text: Spices boiled in 80ml water, tea in for 2 minutes, honey dissolved. Cool 10 minutes.
steps.cue: Concentrate dark as cola

steps.title: Build cold
steps.text: Concentrate over packed ice, cold milk on top.
steps.cue: Amber and white marbling

rescues.fix: Concentrate was single-strength - the ice always wins unless you brew double

name: Air Fryer Potatoes
tagline: Crisp outside, fluffy inside, minimal oil.

steps.title: Soak and dry
steps.text: Potato wedges soaked 15 min, patted bone dry.

steps.title: Air fry
steps.text: 200 C, 20 min, shake twice. Oil and spices last 5 min.

rescues.fix: They were wet - drying is non-negotiable
Cut smaller; 20 min assumes half-inch wedges

name: Aloo Gobi
tagline: Potato and cauliflower, dry and spiced - the side that eats like a main.

steps.title: Par-cook
steps.text: Potato and cauliflower with turmeric, salt, splash water. Cover 8 min.

steps.title: Dry roast
steps.text: Lid off, oil, jeera, chilli. Toss till speckled gold and dry.

rescues.fix: Cauliflower overcooked - add it 3 min after potato
High heat lid-off till steam escapes

name: Boondi Raita
tagline: Crunchy boondi in cold curd - the chole companion.

steps.title: Whisk curd
steps.text: Smooth curd, jeera, kala namak.

steps.title: Add boondi last
steps.text: Boondi just before serving - soak 2 min max for crunch.

rescues.fix: Add boondi at the table, not 20 min ahead
Thin with cold water - raita should pour

name: Bruschetta
tagline: Toasted bread, raw tomato, summer on a crostini.

steps.title: Toast
steps.text: Bread golden, garlic rub while hot.

steps.title: Top
steps.text: Tomato, basil, olive oil, salt. Serve immediately - bread waits for no one.

rescues.fix: Dice tomato, drain 5 min before topping
Salt the tomato separately - it needs its own seasoning

name: Caprese Salad
tagline: Tomato, mozzarella, basil - the Italian flag on a plate.

steps.title: Layer
steps.text: Tomato, mozzarella, basil - alternate on a plate.

steps.title: Dress
steps.text: Olive oil, salt, pepper. Room temp tomatoes taste best.

rescues.fix: Salt tomatoes separately, drain 2 min before plating
Good olive oil and flaky salt - caprese is an ingredient dish

name: Charred Corn
tagline: Smoky kernels, lime squeeze, street-food nostalgia.

steps.title: Char
steps.text: Dry pan or grill, corn till blackened in spots. Butter brush optional.

steps.title: Cut and season
steps.text: Kernels off cob, lime, chilli, coriander. Serve warm.

rescues.fix: Pan not hot enough - dry pan, no oil first
Fresh corn chars better than old frozen

name: Garlic Parmesan Baby Potatoes
tagline: Crisp potatoes, garlic butter, parmesan snow.

steps.title: Par-boil
steps.text: Potatoes 8 min till fork-tender. Drain rough for fuzzy edges.

steps.title: Crisp
steps.text: Air fryer 200 C, 15 min with oil. Butter-garlic toss, parmesan snow.

rescues.fix: Rough the boiled edges - fuzz = crunch
Add garlic after cooking, in warm butter only

name: Guacamole
tagline: An avocado's finest hour, minus the cafe markup.

spice.dial_up: Extra green chilli, seeds in
spice.dial_down: Skip the chilli entirely

ingredients.display: 1 ripe half avocado (80g) - it should give like a stress ball
juice of a third of a lime - also the anti-browning insurance

steps.title: Mash coarse
steps.text: Fork, not blender. Leave lumps - guac is a texture, not a paste.
steps.cue: Half smooth, half chunks

steps.title: Fold and season
steps.text: Onion, tomato, chilli, coriander, lime, salt. Fold twice, taste, salt again probably.
steps.cue: Bright green, flecked, tastes louder than its parts

rescues.fix: Press cling film onto the surface, more lime - oxygen is the enemy
Tomato seeds got in - deseed properly, drain and refold

host.plating: Molcajete energy: rough bowl, lime wedge on the rim
host.prep_ahead: Prep the mix-ins ahead - mash the avocado at the last possible minute

name: Jeera Rice
tagline: The supporting actor every gravy calls first.

leftovers_become.note: cold jeera rice is a head start
mash with curd, retemper

steps.title: Bloom
steps.text: Ghee on medium, jeera in till it crackles and darkens a shade.
steps.cue: Jeera smells toasty, not burnt

steps.title: Steam
steps.text: Drained rice, salt, 1.5x water by volume. Lid on lowest flame 12 min, rest 5.
steps.cue: Surface has steam holes, grains stand alone

rescues.fix: Rinse harder next time - the starch is the enemy
Two spoons hot water, lid back on, 4 more minutes

name: Masala Popcorn
tagline: Movie night with chaat masala energy.

steps.title: Pop
steps.text: Heavy pot, ghee, 3 test kernels then rest. Shake when popping slows.

steps.title: Season
steps.text: Chilli and kala namak tossed immediately in hot pot.

rescues.fix: Lower heat after first pop - patience beats high flame
Old kernels - fresh popcorn pops better

name: Mint Raita
tagline: Cold, green, and louder than its three ingredients.

variants.name: Cucumber raita
variants.swaps: Swap mint for 80g grated cucumber, squeezed

ingredients.display: half tsp jeera, roasted and crushed

steps.title: Whisk
steps.text: Curd till completely smooth, then mint, jeera, kala namak folded through.
steps.cue: No lumps, pale green streaks

steps.title: Chill
steps.text: Ten minutes in the fridge if you can - raita is a cold dish.
steps.cue: Flavors settle, mint blooms

rescues.fix: Whisk it back in, or start with thicker curd
Stems got in - leaves only, always

aka: tzatziki (add grated cucumber and a drizzle of olive oil)

name: Roasted Vegetables
tagline: Sheet-pan rainbow that makes dinner look intentional.

steps.title: Chop even
steps.text: Same-size pieces roast evenly. Do not crowd the pan.

steps.title: Roast
steps.text: 200 C oven or air fryer 22 min till edges char.

rescues.fix: Pan overcrowded - use two trays
Smaller cuts or lower heat longer

name: Tzatziki
tagline: Cucumber yogurt dip - raita's Mediterranean passport.

steps.title: Drain cucumber
steps.text: Grate, salt, squeeze dry - wet tzatziki is sad tzatziki.

steps.title: Mix and chill
steps.text: Yogurt, cucumber, garlic, lemon, herbs. Fridge 30 min minimum.

rescues.fix: Cucumber not squeezed enough - wring it hard
Half clove only; garlic blooms as it sits
